candidates.exposure_rationale: 100% of revenue from LNG carrier operations across 13 vessels; 3 vessels trading spot at $250k+/day into the Qatar-disrupted market vs $42k/day pre-disruption; 2–3 charter renewals due within the thesis horizon expected to reprice from ~$73k/day to $100k+/day, adding a quantified $20M–$42M EBITDA step-up (7.5–16% above the $267.5M guidance midpoint) that no current sell-side model reflects — the specific variant mechanism.
candidates.liquidity_note: NYSE mid-cap ~$1.59B; active listing confirmed [StockAnalysis.com, 2026-06-26]; adequate daily volume for institutional sizing; 14.5% short interest as of 2026-06-12 creates squeeze potential but also a real borrow cost.
candidates.caveats: Sell consensus (4 Strong Sell, 2 Hold; target $24.92 vs $29.48 current) — sell-side is already below the stock price, which is the variant's point; if Qatar restarts early (falsifier SF-003: shipping rate below $100k/day), the stock has large downside as it trades above all analyst targets. 14.5% short interest is a real borrow cost. Charter-renewal rate assumption ($100k–$130k/day) is inferred from current spot market, not yet a contracted number — the variant crystallises only if a new charter is announced at that rate.

news_impact.missing_reason: No verified filed analyst EV/EBITDA forward model for FLNG available in M0.6.2; forward multiple self-anchored from sector band (unverified web, labelled).
news_impact.event_quantification: FY2026 EBITDA guidance raised to $255–$280M (midpoint $267.5M, +~$17.5M / +11%) following Qatar disruption [Flex LNG 6-K Q1 2026, SEC EDGAR, May 13 2026]. Revenue guidance lifted to $345–$370M (midpoint $357.5M), approximately 10% above February 2026 guidance. Q1 2026 fleet-wide TCE $65,729/day; FY2026 guided TCE $73,000–$78,000/day; 91.2% of remaining 2026 fleet days fixed at contracted rates; 3 open vessels fixed at elevated spot/short-term rates. After-tax margin: FY2025 adjusted net income $101.1M on revenue $340M → 29.7% [Flex LNG Q4 2025 Earnings Release, Nasdaq, 2026-02-11, unverified web, labelled]. Revenue-to-net route: $37.5M revenue uplift × 29.7% = $11.1M ΔE/yr. Duration: 0.5 years (outage window to mid-August 2026). NPV = $11.1M ÷ (1 + 11.5%)^0.5 ≈ $10.5M.
news_impact.delta_value: $10.5M NPV (finite-life NPV of $11.1M/yr × 0.5 yr at 11.5% cost of equity). Duration-trap check: capitalising $11.1M at 16.2× (implied from market cap $1,643M ÷ FY2025 adjusted net income $101.1M) would give $180M — a 17× overstatement of the finite-life event.
news_impact.caveats: Duration trap confirmed — capitalising the guidance-raise earnings at the market multiple overstates value by 17×; NPV method is mandatory for the finite-life classification. The ~35-percentage-point gap reflects: (a) the charter-renewal step-up hypothesis (market pricing $100k+/day renewals — not yet a filed number); (b) momentum re-rate on the LNG shipping narrative; (c) short-squeeze dynamics from 14.5% short interest in a stock trading 19% above the sell-side consensus target. All three are beyond what the fundamentals floor captures. This is a fundamentals floor, not a verdict — the charter-renewal judgment belongs to the research swarm. No price target issued (CLAUDE.md §16).

candidates.exposure_rationale: 100% of total operating revenue from LNG infrastructure after complete exit from legacy LNG carrier operations in 2025; Q1 2026 revenues $137.6M entirely from FLNG Hilli (Cameroon, tolling with variable LNG-price-linked fee component) and FLNG Gimi (BP 20-year lease, started mid-2025); captures DIR-002 mechanism via tolling fees with partial LNG-price linkage on the Hilli contract [Golar LNG 6-K Q1 2026, SEC EDGAR, 2026].
candidates.liquidity_note: NASDAQ mid-cap ~$5.07B; active listing confirmed [StockAnalysis.com, 2026-06-26]; Strong Buy consensus; 6.05% short interest as of 2026-05-29 with recent covering activity.
candidates.caveats: The LNG-price link within Hilli's tolling formula is not confirmed from filed contract terms — the exact pass-through rate is unknown. FLNG Gimi revenue is fixed under the BP sales-type lease (high reported margin but accounting construct, not traditional repeat cash flow). The mechanism reaches earnings more slowly and with more contractual buffering than FLNG's spot-sensitive vessels. The market is pricing Golar as a floating liquefaction platform operator in a tighter LNG world — a re-rate story beyond what the closed guidance data confirms.

news_impact.missing_reason: Hilli variable tolling-fee formula and LNG-price-link percentage not confirmed from filed contract terms; Gimi revenue fixed under sales-type lease. Conservative inference used: 20% of $258M annualised EBITDA uplift attributed to LNG-price-linked Hilli variable component.
news_impact.event_quantification: Q1 2026 net income $83.6M on revenues $137.6M (60.7% after-tax margin; note: FLNG Gimi sales-type lease accounting inflates reported margin — true operating margin on Hilli tolling unconfirmed) [Golar LNG 6-K Q1 2026, SEC EDGAR, 2026; Quiver Quantitative / Investing.com Q1 2026 summary, 2026-06-28, unverified web, labelled]. Conservative Qatar-attributable ΔE/yr: 20% × $258M annualised EBITDA uplift × 60.7% after-tax = ~$31.3M/yr (inference basis, not from filed contract terms). Duration: 0.5 years. NPV: $31.3M/yr × 0.5 yr ÷ (1 + 11.5%)^0.5 ≈ $14.9M (upper indicative bound).
news_impact.delta_value: $14.9M NPV (upper indicative bound, inference basis). Duration-trap check: capitalising $31.3M at implied P/E of 15.2× (market cap ~$5,070M ÷ annualised net income $334.4M) gives $476M → $476M ÷ $5,070M = 9.4% implied move — a 31× overstatement vs the NPV-based floor of 0.3%.
news_impact.caveats: The 20% attribution of EBITDA uplift to price-linked Hilli fees is inference — research swarm should verify the Hilli variable-fee formula from the filed 6-K or prior annual report. Duration trap confirmed (31× overstatement if capitalised at the P/E). FLNG Gimi's sales-type lease accounting inflates the reported after-tax margin. The observed ~24% move reflects floating-liquefaction platform optionality and narrative re-rate, not the discrete tolling-fee uplift this floor captures. This is a fundamentals floor — no price target issued.

candidates.exposure_rationale: ~51% of Q1 2026 LNG sold on gas-hub index pricing (JKM/TTF-linked), directly capturing the spot-price uplift from Qatar's 12.8 mtpa offline capacity [Rigzone, 2026-04-29, unverified web, labelled; Woodside Q1 2026 ASX quarterly]; Scarborough first cargo targeted Q4 2026 (96% complete as of Q1 2026) adds additional volume into the elevated market; the remaining 49% on long-term contracts dilutes the mechanism; company deliberately insulated from shipping rate spike (term shipping, no Strait of Hormuz routing).
candidates.liquidity_note: NYSE large-cap ~$36.3B; dual-listed NYSE and ASX (primary); highly liquid; no suspension. AUD/USD FX exposure — any model needs the period-appropriate rate.
candidates.caveats: 49% of LNG volumes on long-term contracts — significant dilution. Captures DIR-002 price uplift but not DIR-001 rate premium (term shipping strategy). Falsifier SF-002 (TTF below $10.96/MMBtu) hits WDS's gas-hub-indexed volumes directly. Stock has retraced from its March 2026 high; current price ~$20.05 is well below the disruption peak of $25.19. AUD/USD FX must be tracked in any model.

news_impact.missing_reason: Woodside hedge ratios for H1 2026 not confirmed from filed disclosures in this run; Qatar-attribution of realised price uplift is inference (50% of the 11% Q1 realised price lift attributed to Qatar-linked LNG spot repricing, balance attributed to non-LNG commodity mix and volume timing).
news_impact.event_quantification: FY2025 revenue $12,984M; after-tax margin 20.9% (NPAT $2,718M ÷ revenue $12,984M) [Woodside FY2025 Full-Year Results Press Release, February 24 2026, Woodside IR / Business Wire]. Q1 2026 operating revenue $3.26B; average realised price +11% QoQ [Woodside Q1 2026 ASX quarterly]. ~51% spot-linked → $6,622M annual revenue base. Conservative Qatar attribution: 5.5% × $6,622M × 20.9% = $76M ΔE/yr. Duration: 0.5 years → ΔE $38M. NPV = $38M ÷ (1 + 11.5%)^0.5 ≈ $36M.
news_impact.delta_value: $36M NPV ($38M ÷ 1.056). Duration-trap check: capitalising $76M at 14.4× P/E (market cap ~$38,100M ÷ underlying NPAT $2,649M) gives $1,094M → +2.9%; NPV-based floor is +0.09%; overstatement ~32×. Upper bound (full 51% price pass-through, no hedges): $12,984M × 51% × 51% × 20.9% ÷ 2 ÷ 1.056 = ~$334M NPV → +0.88% — still well below the observed event-day move.
news_impact.caveats: Duration trap confirmed (32× overstatement if capitalised at the P/E). The 5.5% conservative Qatar attribution is inference — true figure depends on hedge ratios not confirmed from filings. Even the upper bound (+0.88%) does not approach the observed +6.8% event-day move. The excess reflects sector narrative repricing, oil/LNG correlation flows, and Scarborough volume optionality entering an elevated-price environment. This is a fundamentals floor — no price target issued.

candidates.exposure_rationale: Largest US LNG exporter (Sabine Pass + Corpus Christi); ~5% of production is uncontracted optimization volume selling at JKM/TTF + basis (the spot-price uplift mechanism); 95% under long-term SPAs with ~15-year average remaining life dilutes spot exposure and insulates most of the business from thesis downside; FY2026 Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA guidance raised $500M at midpoint to $7.25–7.75B (midpoint $7.50B), explicitly citing the Qatar disruption [Cheniere Energy 8-K Q1 2026, SEC EDGAR, May 7 2026].
candidates.liquidity_note: NYSE large-cap ~$50.6B; most liquid name in this set; Strong Buy consensus (21 analysts Buy or Strong Buy); 1.90% short interest; no liquidity concern.
candidates.caveats: Near-pure long-term-contract book means only the marginal ~5% optimization volume captures the spot uplift — thesis impact is real but small relative to total EBITDA. Management already guided for the Qatar windfall; guidance is already in consensus. The news-impact sizing shows the stock is an underpriced_candidate at current prices: the guidance-raise floor implies ~$285 fair value vs the current price of $241.64 (approximately 15% below the floor). The Q1 2026 non-cash derivative loss of $5.4B is a §15 reporting artefact — does not affect adjusted earnings. Incremental variant signal here is thin vs FLNG.

news_impact.event_quantification: FY2026 Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA guidance raised $500M at midpoint (from $6.75–7.25B to $7.25–7.75B, midpoint $7.50B); DCF guidance raised $500M at midpoint to $4.75–5.25B [Cheniere Energy 8-K Q1 2026, SEC EDGAR, May 7 2026]. Q1 2026 consolidated adjusted EBITDA $2.33B (+25% YoY); revenues $5.87B; adjusted net income $1.01B. EBITDA-to-net conversion ratio: $1.01B ÷ $2.33B = 43.3%. ΔE from guidance raise: $500M × 43.3% = ~$216M additional adjusted net income for FY2026. EPS revision: $216M ÷ 209.55M shares = +$1.03 / +5.8% vs NTM EPS mean $17.91 [StockAnalysis.com, June 12 2026, unverified web, labelled]. Note: reported Q1 EPS distorted by $5.4B non-cash derivative loss — analysis uses adjusted figures throughout (§15). ΔM = 0 (no justified multiple expansion from finite-window guidance raise; sector EV/EBITDA held at 10.2×; 8×–12× sector normal range, M0.6.2 Block 3).
news_impact.delta_value: Primary-mode: (1 + 5.8%)(1 + 0%) − 1 = +5.8%. Duration-trap check: applying $500M EBITDA raise at 10.2× EV/EBITDA gives $5.1B EV increase → +10.1% on $50.6B market cap — overstates by treating the finite-window guidance raise as a permanent run-rate; the +5.8% ΔE-only method (multiple held constant) is the correct floor. The $285 fair-value floor = pre-disruption reference $269 × 1.058.
news_impact.caveats: At the March 19, 2026 event-day reaction (+6.0% observed vs +5.8% implied), the market priced the guidance raise almost exactly — gap read is 'priced' at the event date. But the stock has since fallen to $241.64, approximately 10.1% below the pre-disruption $269 reference, while the $7.50B EBITDA guidance remains in force. The fundamentals floor implies a fair-value level of ~$285 — making the stock an underpriced_candidate at current prices by this metric alone. The Q1 non-cash derivative loss ($5.4B) depresses the reported P/E but does not affect adjusted earnings. ΔM = 0 is deliberately conservative; if the IEA 3–5 year outage scenario materialises, re-rate from 10.2× toward 11–12× EV/EBITDA would raise the implied move to 16–26% from the current price. The research swarm should verify the durable proportion of the EBITDA raise (guidance partially includes a non-recurring tax credit). This is a fundamentals floor — no price target issued.

candidates.exposure_rationale: Operates a fleet of FSRUs (floating storage and regasification units) under fixed-rate take-or-pay time-charter contracts; IND-002 mechanism (higher structural LNG import demand from the European storage deficit supporting FSRU re-contracting at elevated rates) reaches P&L only at contract renewal events, not through current cashflows; Q1 2026 revenue $433M; FY2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance $515–545M [Excelerate Energy 10-Q Q1 2026, SEC EDGAR, 2026].
candidates.liquidity_note: NYSE mid-cap ~$4.29B; ~1M shares average daily volume (adequate for institutional sizing); 99.8% fleet reliability in Q1 2026; no suspension found.
candidates.caveats: Low exposure score because existing contracts are fixed-rate — the thesis only reaches earnings at re-contracting. The re-contracting timeline within the M0.4 horizon (medium, weeks to 3 months) is uncertain; the mechanism is real over a 12–24 month window but may not resolve within the current thesis expiry (mid-August 2026). Treat as a longer-horizon expression of the LNG import demand story.

news_impact.missing_reason: EE's existing FSRU fleet operates on fixed-rate take-or-pay time-charter contracts — the IND-002 mechanism (higher LNG import demand supporting FSRU re-contracting at elevated rates) reaches EE's P&L only at contract renewal, not through current cashflows [Excelerate Energy 10-Q Q1 2026, SEC EDGAR]. No specific contract renewal or charter announcement attributable to the Qatar disruption has been filed or publicly disclosed as of 2026-06-28. Sizing an unconfirmed forward re-contracting event would require assumptions on timing, rates, and terms not in any filing.
news_impact.event_quantification: Not applicable — no quantifiable closed event for EE from the Qatar disruption within the current reporting window; the IND-002 thesis mechanism accrues at the next re-contracting event, which may fall outside the mid-August 2026 thesis horizon.
news_impact.caveats: Not applicable. This is a forward re-contracting optionality story; the fundamentals-floor framework applies only to current-period earnings impacts.

candidates.exposure_rationale: Spain's regulated gas transmission system operator; also operates three regasification terminals and three underground storage facilities (33 TWh working capacity); both the DIR-003 storage mechanism and the IND-002 regasification throughput mechanism reach this company's P&L through regulated throughput tariffs and capacity reservation fees; storage segment revenue not separately confirmed from filing; FY2025 recurring EBITDA €675.7M [Enagás FY2025 results press release, enagas.es, 2026].
candidates.liquidity_note: Active BME listing (ticker: ENG) confirmed [StockAnalysis.com BME:ENG, 2026-06-26]; price €17.61; market cap ~€4.58B (mid-cap); Hold consensus (20 analysts; target €16.85, slight downside); no US ADR found — US investors need BME access or a European broker.
candidates.caveats: Access constraint: no US ADR means US institutional investors must use a European broker or BME access. Regulated Spanish tariff framework (CNMC) caps the storage and regasification upside the thesis relies on. Enagás also holds a stake in Tallgrass Energy (US pipeline) — adds non-thesis dilution. Storage segment revenue not confirmed from filed segment tables — exposure is directional only until FY2025 Annual Report segment tables are reviewed.

news_impact.missing_reason: Enagás' storage and regasification revenues are regulated under the Spanish CNMC tariff framework. The tariff structure caps the uplift from the storage deficit and elevated LNG import demand; the exact regulation-determined capacity fee change for the 2026 injection season requires the CNMC's specific tariff order, which was not retrieved. Storage segment revenue not confirmed as a discrete filing-level line in the upstream candidate inputs.
news_impact.event_quantification: Not applicable — regulated tariff structure prevents direct conversion of DIR-003 mechanism to a filing-confirmed revenue uplift for the event period.
news_impact.caveats: Not applicable. Sizing requires the CNMC tariff order and confirmed storage segment revenue.

candidates.exposure_rationale: Enel's Italian CCGT (gas-fired) generation faces the HARM-002 mechanism — gas sets the marginal electricity price in ~89% of hours in Italy in 2026, so TTF/PSV spot price increases (+35%) transmit directly to Enel's unhedged fuel procurement cost; but Enel is a $115.6B diversified utility with 60%+ of MW capacity in renewables plus international networks and retail — the unhedged Italian CCGT gas procurement is a fraction of group earnings and is not quantified from the filed annual report.
candidates.liquidity_note: OTC ADR (ENLAY): low liquidity, wide spreads; ENLAY price $11.44. Primary listing BIT:ENEL on Borsa Italiana is large-cap and liquid ($115.6B market cap). US investors face practical constraints via OTC; primary access is through BIT:ENEL or a European broker.
candidates.caveats: Low exposure score (18/100) — HARM-002 mechanism not quantified at the issuer level; marked as directional exposure only. Heavy diversification (renewables, grids, international) means the gas-cost squeeze is a small fraction of group EBITDA. OTC ADR (ENLAY) has low liquidity; BIT:ENEL is the practical access route. Treat as illustrative short leg only unless segment-level hedge disclosure confirms material unhedged CCGT gas exposure. The HARM-001 short thesis (spot LNG importers) has no investable listed expression — ENEL is the closest available short leg despite poor quantification. Pair trade (FLNG long / ENEL short) is weak on the short side; size carefully or treat as sector hedge.

news_impact.missing_reason: Enel's unhedged Italian CCGT gas procurement volume and hedge ratio are not confirmed from the filed FY2025 Annual Report (BIT:ENEL, Borsa Italiana). Without the specific CCGT-fired generation volume, hedge ratio, and TTF reference price in Enel's procurement contracts, the cost-harm ΔE cannot be quantified. Flagged as 'exposure not yet quantified' in both upstream agents.
news_impact.event_quantification: Not applicable — exposure not quantifiable without hedge ratio and segment disclosure from filings.
news_impact.caveats: Not applicable. Research swarm should pull the CCGT segment from Enel's FY2025 Annual Report (BIT:ENEL, Borsa Italiana) before sizing this name.
